title: ST 558 HW 4 - Chris’s version
text:
library(httr)

Warning: package 'httr' was built under R version 4.5.3

library(jsonlite)

Warning: package 'jsonlite' was built under R version 4.5.3

title: ST 558 HW 4 - Chris’s version
text:
Exploring Categorical Variables:

Contingency Tables with table()

table(rcs$AgeGroup)


 1:Older 2:Recent 3:Newest 
      69      190      376 

table(rcs$AgeGroup, rcs$Scale)

          
           Extreme Family Kiddie Thrill
  1:Older       17     13      5     34
  2:Recent     105     40      9     36
  3:Newest     147    125     14     90

# interpreting this one Vvv
table(rcs$AgeGroup, rcs$Scale, rcs$Type)

, ,  = Steel

          
           Extreme Family Kiddie Thrill
  1:Older        8      6      4     18
  2:Recent      82     37      9     27
  3:Newest     126    120     14     79

, ,  = Wood

          
           Extreme Family Kiddie Thrill
  1:Older        9      7      1     16
  2:Recent      23      3      0      9
  3:Newest      21      5      0     11


The value “16” in the second level of the two way table implies that there are 16 older wooden thrill rides in the dataset.


Conditional Tables

# way one - subsetting the data
wood_rcs <- filter(rcs, Type == "Wood")
table(wood_rcs$AgeGroup, wood_rcs$Scale)

          
           Extreme Family Kiddie Thrill
  1:Older        9      7      1     16
  2:Recent      23      3      0      9
  3:Newest      21      5      0     11

# way two - subsetting a three way table
table(rcs$AgeGroup, rcs$Scale, rcs$Type)[,,2]

          
           Extreme Family Kiddie Thrill
  1:Older        9      7      1     16
  2:Recent      23      3      0      9
  3:Newest      21      5      0     11




Contingency Tables with group_by() and summarize()

dplyr_table <- rcs |>
               select(AgeGroup, Scale, Type) |>
               group_by(Type, AgeGroup, Scale) |>
               dplyr::summarize(count = n()) |>
               pivot_wider(names_from = Scale, values_from = count)

`summarise()` has grouped output by 'Type', 'AgeGroup'. You can override using
the `.groups` argument.

dplyr_table

# A tibble: 6 × 6
# Groups:   Type, AgeGroup [6]
  Type  AgeGroup Extreme Family Kiddie Thrill
  <fct> <fct>      <int>  <int>  <int>  <int>
1 Steel 1:Older        8      6      4     18
2 Steel 2:Recent      82     37      9     27
3 Steel 3:Newest     126    120     14     79
4 Wood  1:Older        9      7      1     16
5 Wood  2:Recent      23      3     NA      9
6 Wood  3:Newest      21      5     NA     11




Stacked and Side-by-side Bar Graphs
Stacked plots are the default option, so we just use geom_bar()

library(ggplot2)

stacked_bar <- ggplot(rcs, aes(x = Scale, fill = Type)) +
               geom_bar() +
               labs(title = "Count By Roller Coaster Scale",
                    subtitle = "Stacked Bars",
                    y = "Frequency") +
               theme_minimal()

stacked_bar








To get the bars to dodge, we just have to specify the position argument:

library(ggplot2)

sbs_bar <- ggplot(rcs, aes(x = Scale, fill = Type)) +
           geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
           labs(title = "Count By Roller Coaster Scale",
                subtitle = "Side-by-side Bars",
                y = "Frequency") +
           theme_minimal()

sbs_bar

title: ST 558 HW 4 - Chris’s version
text:
Exploring Numeric Variables

Center + Spread for two variables
Now, we’re looking at the mean and standard deviation for TopSpeed and MaxHeight:

cntr_sprd_tbl <- rcs |>
                 select(TopSpeed, MaxHeight) |>
                 drop_na() |> # drop missing due to high missingness for TopSpeed
                 summarize(across(everything(),
                                  list(mean = mean, sd = sd)))

cntr_sprd_tbl

# A tibble: 1 × 4
  TopSpeed_mean TopSpeed_sd MaxHeight_mean MaxHeight_sd
          <dbl>       <dbl>          <dbl>        <dbl>
1          42.4        19.4           84.3         61.3


Now subset to only include steel coasters:

cntr_sprd_tbl_steel <- rcs |>
                       filter(Type == "Steel") |>
                       select(TopSpeed, MaxHeight) |>
                       drop_na() |> # drop missing due to high missingness for TopSpeed
                       summarize(across(everything(),
                                        list(mean = mean, sd = sd)))

cntr_sprd_tbl_steel

# A tibble: 1 × 4
  TopSpeed_mean TopSpeed_sd MaxHeight_mean MaxHeight_sd
          <dbl>       <dbl>          <dbl>        <dbl>
1          40.8        20.6           84.3         66.3




Center + Spread for two variables + Grouping

cntr_sprd_1grp <- rcs |>
                  select(Type, TopSpeed, MaxHeight) |>
                  group_by(Type) |>
                  drop_na() |> # drop missing due to high missingness for TopSpeed
                  summarize(across(everything(),
                                  list(mean = mean, sd = sd)))

cntr_sprd_1grp

# A tibble: 2 × 5
  Type  TopSpeed_mean TopSpeed_sd MaxHeight_mean MaxHeight_sd
  <fct>         <dbl>       <dbl>          <dbl>        <dbl>
1 Steel          40.8        20.6           84.3         66.3
2 Wood           49.3        10.6           84.2         30.8



cntr_sprd_2grp <- rcs |>
                  select(Type, Scale, TopSpeed, MaxHeight) |>
                  group_by(Type, Scale) |>
                  drop_na() |> # drop missing due to high missingness for TopSpeed
                  summarize(across(everything(),
                                  list(mean = mean, sd = sd)))

`summarise()` has grouped output by 'Type'. You can override using the
`.groups` argument.

cntr_sprd_2grp

# A tibble: 8 × 6
# Groups:   Type [2]
  Type  Scale   TopSpeed_mean TopSpeed_sd MaxHeight_mean MaxHeight_sd
  <fct> <fct>           <dbl>       <dbl>          <dbl>        <dbl>
1 Steel Extreme          57.3       14.3           133.          62.9
2 Steel Family           20.9       10.7            30.9         25.6
3 Steel Kiddie           10.3        8.77           15.8         25.1
4 Steel Thrill           34.3        8.63           55.9         22.6
5 Wood  Extreme          55.4        6.56          103.          26.8
6 Wood  Family           32.6        9.61           45           20.5
7 Wood  Kiddie           50         NA              60           NA  
8 Wood  Thrill           45.9        8.34           69.8         15.2


As we saw previously, there isn’t enough data to estimate the height and speed of the wooden kiddie coasters.


Correlation Matrix

rcs_numeric <- select(rcs, where(is.numeric)) |>
               drop_na()


cor(rcs_numeric)

               Year.Opened   TopSpeed MaxHeight      Drop      Length  Duration
Year.Opened     1.00000000 0.07523024 0.1744486 0.1488023 -0.05230856 0.0191047
TopSpeed        0.07523024 1.00000000 0.8995804 0.9106544  0.72958067 0.3396941
MaxHeight       0.17444865 0.89958035 1.0000000 0.9588304  0.60634255 0.2666018
Drop            0.14880229 0.91065440 0.9588304 1.0000000  0.64206458 0.2665382
Length         -0.05230856 0.72958067 0.6063425 0.6420646  1.00000000 0.5069320
Duration        0.01910470 0.33969411 0.2666018 0.2665382  0.50693203 1.0000000
Num.Inversions  0.16157407 0.33770901 0.3102168 0.2957917  0.19466332 0.1094986
               Num.Inversions
Year.Opened         0.1615741
TopSpeed            0.3377090
MaxHeight           0.3102168
Drop                0.2957917
Length              0.1946633
Duration            0.1094986
Num.Inversions      1.0000000




Histogram

top_spd_hist <- ggplot(rcs |> select(Type, TopSpeed) |> drop_na(), aes(x = TopSpeed, fill = Type)) +
                geom_histogram() +
                labs(title = "Top Speed by Ride Type",
                     x = "Top Speed (MpH)",
                     y = "Frequency")

max_hgt_hist <- ggplot(rcs |> select(Type, MaxHeight) |> drop_na(), aes(x = MaxHeight, fill = Type)) +
                geom_histogram() +
                labs(title = "Maximum Height by Ride Type",
                     x = "Height (ft.)",
                     y = "Frequency")


top_spd_hist

`stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.








max_hgt_hist

`stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.











Density Plots

top_spd_density <- ggplot(rcs |> select(Type, TopSpeed) |> drop_na(), aes(x = TopSpeed, fill = Type)) +
                   geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
                   labs(title = "Distribution of Top Speed by Ride Type",
                        x = "Top Speed (MpH)",
                        y = "Frequency")

max_hgt_density <- ggplot(rcs |> select(Type, MaxHeight) |> drop_na(), aes(x = MaxHeight, fill = Type)) +
                   geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
                   labs(title = "Distribution of Maximum Height by Ride Type",
                        x = "Height (ft.)",
                        y = "Frequency")


top_spd_density







max_hgt_density










Box Plots

top_spd_box <- ggplot(rcs |> select(Type, TopSpeed) |> drop_na(), aes(x = TopSpeed, fill = Type)) +
               geom_boxplot() +
               labs(title = "Distribution of Top Speed by Ride Type",
                        x = "Top Speed (MpH)",
                        y = "Frequency")

max_hgt_box <- ggplot(rcs |> select(Type, MaxHeight) |> drop_na(), aes(x = MaxHeight, fill = Type)) +
               geom_boxplot() +
               labs(title = "Distribution of Maximum Height by Ride Type",
                        x = "Height (ft.)",
                        y = "Frequency")


top_spd_box







max_hgt_box










Scatterplots

topspd_maxhgt <- ggplot(rcs |> drop_na(), aes(x = MaxHeight, y = TopSpeed, color = Type)) +
                 geom_point() +
                 labs(title = "Top Speed by Max Height",
                      x = "Max Height (Feet)",
                      y = "Top Speed (MPH)") +
                 theme_minimal()


topspd_maxhgt










Faceting:

topspd_maxhgt_1fct <- ggplot(rcs |> drop_na(), aes(x = MaxHeight, y = TopSpeed)) +
                      geom_point() +
                      labs(title = "Top Speed by Max Height",
                               x = "Max Height (Feet)",
                               y = "Top Speed (MPH)") +
                      facet_wrap(~Type, nrow = 2) +
                      theme_minimal()

topspd_maxhgt_1fct









topspd_maxhgt_2fct <- ggplot(rcs |> drop_na(), aes(x = MaxHeight, y = TopSpeed)) +
                      geom_point() +
                      labs(title = "Top Speed by Max Height",
                               x = "Max Height (Feet)",
                               y = "Top Speed (MPH)") +
                      facet_wrap(vars(Type, Scale)) +
                      theme_minimal()

topspd_maxhgt_2fct
